business.headline: Delhi power bills may rise after tribunal rejects plea on Rs 30,000 crore duesThe dues are linked to pending recoveries payable to Delhi's power distribution companies (discoms) under a larger liquidation plan aimed at clearing long-standing liabilities in the power sector.

Big relief for shipping industry: India clears Rs 13,000 crore insurance poolIndia's shipping sector has long depended on global insurance markets to keep goods moving across oceans. But recent disruptions in key trade routes and rising geopolitical risks have exposed a critical gap.

ICICI Bank rises nearly 2% after Q4 results: Should you buy now?ICICI Bank reported a net profit of about Rs 13,702 crore for the fourth quarter, up 8.5% from a year ago. It also announced a dividend of Rs 12 per share for FY26, higher than the Rs 11 per share declared last year, adding to investor confidence.

HDFC Bank drops 1% after Q4 results: Profit rises, so why is stock down?HDFC Bank reported a net profit of about Rs 19,221 crore for the fourth quarter, up around 9% from a year ago. But shares of India's largest private lender fell in early trade.

Strait of Hormuz crisis returns: Oil prices climb 6% on fresh conflict fearsLatest data shows WTI crude at $88.98, up 6.12%, while Brent crude has climbed to $95.23, up 5.37% as of 9 am. The sudden rise reflects growing concern that oil supply could be hit if tensions continue.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock markets rise or fall today?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 24,469.50 at 7:57 am, indicating that the Nifty 50 may open above Friday's close of 24,353.55.

Why your car insurance may not pay when you need it mostAn accident, a flooded road, a damaged engine, and a car insurance claim is filed with the expectation that the insurer will step in. But for many, that moment turns into something else entirely: confusion, partial payouts, or in some cases, outright rejection.

India's digital payments boom has a growing fraud problemIndia's digital boom has made payments effortless, but also easier to exploit. From fake identities to instant scams, fraudsters are moving faster than the systems meant to stop them, and users are paying the price.

Govt approves 2% dearness allowance hike for central govt employees, pensionersThe Union Cabinet approved a 2% hike in Dearness Allowance for government employees and pensioners, along with key decisions including a Rs 13,000 crore Sovereign Maritime Fund and extension of PMGSY till 2028.

On paper, compliant: How labour laws are quietly underminedIn the third episode in this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines how employers maintain compliance on paper while systematically evading legal obligations in practice.

Digital gold, ETFs or SGBs: Where should you invest this Akshaya Tritiya?Akshaya Tritiya is here, and gold buying is once again in focus. But instead of just buying jewellery, many are now exploring smarter ways to invest. So, which option, digital gold, ETFs or SGBs, makes the most sense?

entertainment.headline: It's a girl: Director Atlee, wife Priya welcome second child Filmmaker Atlee and wife Priya have welcomed their second child, a baby girl, bringing joy to the family. They shared the news with a cute cartoon portrait of their son, Meer, celebrating his new role as big brother.

Samay Raina says backlash left him fearing debt like Amitabh Bachchan Comedian Samay Raina revealed how the backlash from India's Got Latent controversy left him fearing bankruptcy, similar to Amitabh Bachchan's financial crisis in the late 90s.

Watch Actor Dhanush has revealed that the sequel to Vada Chennai will go on floors soon, promising a powerful continuation of the original crime drama. Dipika Kakar says crying eases anxiety, shares update on cancer treatment Dipika Kakar opened up about dealing with anxiety during MRI scans amid her cancer treatment, sharing that crying helps her cope. The actor continues her recovery with close monitoring after her latest scan and is set to begin immunotherapy.

Bhooth Bangla box office Day 3: Rs 100 crore a cakewalk for Akshay's film worldwide Akshay Kumar's Bhooth Bangla is off to a strong start at the box office, racing towards the Rs 100 crore mark within its opening weekend. Here's how the horror-comedy performed day-wise and worldwide.

Daredevil, Punisher, Jessica Jones: MCU street vigilantes deserve film franchises As Marvel navigates post-Phase 4 fatigue and leans heavily on nostalgia with Spider-Man: Brand New Day and Avengers crossovers, is it time to bring the raw, grounded vigilantes like Daredevil, Punisher and Jessica Jones in standalone theatrical releases? Can street heroes outshine MCU's cosmic warriors on the big-screen?

She also said the South offers more meaningful roles for women.

Kara trailer: Dhanush returns as reformed robber in Vignesh Raja's crime drama The trailer of Kara, starring Dhanush, offers a gripping glimpse into a story set against the 1991 Gulf War backdrop. Directed by Vignesh Raja, the film releases in theatres on April 30.

French actor Nathalie Baye, known for Catch Me If You Can, dies at 77 French actor Nathalie Baye, known for playing Leonardo DiCaprio's mother in Catch Me If You Can, died at 77 after battling Lewy body dementia.

Shruti Haasan loses her cool at paps for calling her mumma: What are you saying? Shruti Haasan reacted sharply after a paparazzo called her "mumma" during a public outing, in a moment that quickly went viral. The incident has sparked debate around personal boundaries, with many backing her stance.
Darshan Kumar, T-Series veteran and Gulshan Kumar's brother, dies at 70 Darshan Kumar, younger brother of Gulshan Kumar and a key figure behind T-Series, died in Delhi. He was 70 years old.

Samantha, Katrina to Priyanka: Bollywood showers Deepika-Ranveer with love B-town's favourite couple is expanding their family and the stars are thrilled! Deepika Padukone and Ranveer Singh announced second pregnancy with an adorable post on April 19.

general news.headline: Video: Moment US Marines rappelled onto Iran ship and seized itUS Marines rappelled from helicopters onto the Iranian-flagged vessel Touska in the Arabian Sea after it ignored warnings for six hours, with support from USS Tripoli and USS Spruance, which disabled the ship's propulsion.

Unlike 9/11, Cold War, no windfall now: Pak bleeds as Iran-US crisis deepensHistorically, Pakistan's strategic location brought economic gains, with wars and global alignments yielding grants, debt relief, and reimbursements. But that equation is now reversing, as Gulf tensions trigger capital flight, reserve depletion, and rising repayment pressures.

4 months into marriage, American Express manager dies by suicide in GurugramA 33-year-old American Express manager was found dead in his Gurugram flat, with police suspecting suicide following a late-night argument with his wife.

How Gulf countries paid $142 billion to US for a leaky THAAD umbrellaGulf countries poured billions into American missile defences designed to stop Iran's arsenal. Yet when retaliation came, sheer volume and cost asymmetry turned the equation. The Middle East countries were left with charred THAAD systems and a costly lesson.

Thar, gun, cash: Amravati sex scandal accused's luxury lifestyle under probeIn viral reels, the accused is seen driving high-end vehicles, displaying bundles of cash, holding what appears to be a firearm, and using multiple expensive smartphones. Investigators are now tracing the source of his income and whether the funds are linked to more criminal activity.

Watch: Astronaut records breathtaking Earth setting behind the MoonA stunning iPhone video by Nasa astronaut Reid Wiseman captures Earth disappearing behind the Moon during the historic Artemis 2 mission, offering a rare and personal glimpse of space exploration.

Live: Iran will continue talks with US but 'not at any cost', says top lawmakerUS-Iran-Israel War Live Updates: Iran refused to hold the second round of the ceasefire talks with the United States, citing Washington's "excessive demands, unrealistic expectations, constant shifts in stance, repeated contradictions, and the naval blockade of the maritime chokepoint, Strait of Hormuz. Meanwhile, Donald Trump said that the US delegation team, led by Vice President JD Vance, will arrive in Islamabad today. However, after Iran's refusal, further ceasefire talks regarding the prevailing West Asia conflict look bleak. Amid the developments, Pakistan's Prime Minister, Shehbaz Sharif, held a phone call with Iran's President, Masoud Pezeshkian, and discussed the regional situation. He also briefed Pezeshkian on the conversation the South Asian country had with Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Turkey. Catch all the live updates at Indiatoday.in.

Green card-holding Iranian woman arrested in US for selling arms for TehranProsecutors also accused Shamim Mafi of facilitating deals involving Iranian-made weapons, including drones, bomb fuses and millions of rounds of ammunition destined for Sudan.

Vercel hacked, hacker using ShinyHunters name to sell data for $2 millionVercel, a cloud platform that hosts and deploys web apps, was recently compromised in a cyberattack stemming from an AI tool. The company says that no sensitive data was accessed after hackers claimed to be selling Vercel customer data online. Vercel has clients such as OpenAI, Cursor, Bose, and Pinterest.

On Sunday, Punjab Kings beat the Lucknow Super Giants by 54 runs.

Hit most sixes, win my bat: Shreyas Iyer on PBKS challenge after LSG smash show Shreyas Iyer revealed Punjab Kings have an in-house six-hitting contest, with his bat as the prize, after PBKS smashed 21 sixes against LSG in IPL 2026. PBKS have 63 sixes in six matches so far in the IPL.

Pakistan batter plays only 1 dot ball in PSL centuryPSL 2026: Babar Azam smashed a 52-ball century against Quetta Gladiators, playing just one dot ball in the entire innings. Relieved Ajinkya Rahane jokes of getting 5 extra minutes after KKR's win over RRA relieved Ajinkya Rahane joked that he was happy to get the five extra minutes to conduct the post-match interview after KKR secured their first win of the season against RR.

Rashid Khan turned down citizenship offer from India: I will play for AfghanistanAfghanistan talisman Rashid Khan has revealed that he turned down offers of citizenship from India and Australia, choosing instead to remain committed to representing his country.

How Rinku Singh overcame early brainfade moment to power KKR to first winRinku Singh revealed he had a brief mental lapse during his match-winning knock of 53 against Rajasthan Royals as KKR secured their first IPL 2026 win at Eden Gardens. Rinku finally came into form with his knock of 53.

Shafali Verma, once 15-year-old debutant, now India's youngest to 100 T20IsSAW vs INDW, 2nd T20I: At 22 years and 81 days, Shafali Verma became the youngest Indian to play 100 T20Is, breaking the record of Jemimah Rodrigues.

R Ashwin makes bold predictionIPL 2026: Chennai Super Kings' poor run under Ruturaj Gaikwad has reignited leadership debates, with Ravichandran Ashwin suggesting Sanju Samson could take over captaincy in the near future. With ongoing struggles in big chases and injuries in the squad, CSK will rely on Samson's form and leadership potential moving forward.

KKR vs RR Highlights: Rinku Singh magic hands KKR first win of seasonKolkata (KKR) vs Rajasthan (RR) Highlights, IPL 2026 today match: Rinku Singh magic at the Eden as Kolkata Knight Riders secure their first win of the season by four wickets. education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
